title: cenocline chamaemelifolia
intro: Cenocline chamaemelifolia, also known as the Balsam Apple, is a species of flowering plant in the balsam apple family. It is native to tropical Africa and has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to treat various health issues. The plant has been used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions like eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Cenocline chamaemelifolia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues and fever.
